reasoning: ChargePoint's most recent financial results for Q4 FY2025 (ended January 31, 2025) show an improved liquidity profile and reduced cash burn. As of the end of the fiscal year, the company had $225 million in cash and cash equivalents, along with an undrawn $150 million revolving credit facility, totaling $375 million in liquidity. Furthermore, the company reported that cash used in operating activities plummeted to just $3 million in Q4 2025 (down from $31 million in Q3 2025). Crucially, the company has no debt maturities until 2028. While there has been negative sentiment regarding ChargePoint's stock performance and market position, companies with strong liquidity backing and manageable, short-term debt obligations do not typically file for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. With almost no near-term debt walls and a much-reduced cash burn rate, bankruptcy before July 1, 2025 (less than two months away from the present forecast date) is exceptionally unlikely.
